hook.text: Unhappiness has three parts: un-, happy, -ness. Each piece carries meaning. That's morphology at work.

buildup.text: Morphology studies morphemes — the smallest units of meaning in a language, like prefixes, roots, and suffixes.

discovery.text: Languages differ wildly in morphology. Turkish packs entire sentences into single words through long suffix chains.

twist.text: English is relatively simple morphologically. Mandarin even more so — relying on word order instead of suffixes.

climax.text: Morphology reveals that languages solve the same problems — encoding meaning — in radically different ways.

punchline.text: Meaning hides inside the smallest word parts.

quiz.question: What is a morpheme?
options: The smallest unit of meaning in a language
